title: Tatum Andersen
compiled_truth:
Tatum Andersen is the Founder & CEO of [Nexus Relay](companies/nexus-relay), an AI infrastructure company she started in 2024 after stints at various infrastructure and developer-tools shops. Before founding the company, she spent roughly six years bouncing between mid-stage startups focused on build systems, edge compute, and API management — never staying more than two years at any single one. That restlessness is something she's acknowledged publicly; she's described herself as "allergic to maintenance mode." Once a product ships and stabilizes, she loses interest. That disposition turns out to be useful when you're trying to build something new from scratch.

Nexus Relay focuses on inference-layer infrastructure — the connective tissue between AI models and the applications that consume them. Tatum has been vocal about the idea that model performance means nothing if the delivery pipeline introduces unpredictable latency or data loss. This conviction is what initially brought her into Anchor Codec's orbit. She first appeared on our radar in mid-2026, when she reached out about integrating [Nexus Relay](companies/nexus-relay)'s routing layer with Anchor's single-pass primitives. Her thesis was that deterministic data infrastructure could eliminate an entire class of retry logic that Nexus Relay currently has to maintain.

Andersen is product-obsessed in a way that can be both energizing and exhausting for her team. She's known for writing extremely detailed product specs — sometimes running fifteen pages for a single feature — and then ripping them apart in review. Her analytical instinct means she rarely ships on gut alone; she wants numbers, benchmarks, traces. In conversations she tends to be direct to the point of bluntness, though never unkind. She just doesn't have patience for ambiguity when precision is availble.

Nexus Relay raised a Series A in late 2025 and by early 2027 had grown to around 40 people. Tatum still writes code occasionally, mostly prototypes, and insists on doing her own performance profiling before any integration partner gets access to staging environments. She's based in Denver but travels frequently — we've overlapped with her at multiple infrastructure-focused events and a handful of in-person working sessions. Her reputation in the AI infra community is solid: people respect her technical depth even if they sometimes find her exacting standards hard to meet.
timeline:
- **2026-06-14** | Tatum Andersen first reached out via email about potential integration between [Nexus Relay](companies/nexus-relay) and Anchor's deterministic data primitives.
- **2026-08-02** | Initial technical call with Andersen and two Nexus Relay engineers to discuss single-pass pipeline compatibility.
- **2026-10-19** | Tatum gave a talk at InfraCon Denver titled "Why Retry Logic Is a Smell" — referenced Anchor Codec's approach without naming us directly.
- **2027-01-11** | [Nexus Relay](companies/nexus-relay) completed Series A+1 extension; Tatum mentioned in passing that integration work with Anchor was part of the pitch deck.
- **2027-03-28** | In-person working session in Denver. Andersen personally ran latency benchmarks on the prototype integration over two days.
- **2027-06-05** | Nexus Relay shipped v2.3 of their routing layer with preliminary support for Anchor's single-pass primitives in staging.
- **2027-09-14** | Tatum joined a panel at DataMesh Summit alongside two other Anchor partners; first public acknowledgment of the integration.
- **2028-01-22** | Andersen flagged a subtle edge case in the integration during her own profiling session — led to a patch on our side within 48 hours.
- **2028-04-10** | Nexus Relay announced GA of Anchor-backed routing; Tatum posted a detailed technical breakdown on their engineering blog.

_facts.name: Tatum Andersen
_facts.title: Founder & CEO, Nexus Relay
_facts.background: Founded Nexus Relay in 2024; previously at infrastructure and developer-tools companies
